title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Countess Nicolletti confronts Mrs. Haberdash, accusing her of betraying secrets to a friend and failing to manage the disruptive behavior of the students. The Countess demands the expulsion of a student who admitted to various wrongdoings, lamenting the ruined reputation of the student home. Seeking refuge from the confrontation, Mrs. Haberdash retreats to the kitchen, where the servant Maria appears tense and uncooperative, refusing to make Italian seasoning and offering instead to prepare a pale, English-style stew.

That evening, Mrs. Haberdash attempts to restore peace by gathering the students for dinner. Len Bateson assists Celia by escorting her into the room. The atmosphere is strained; Mr. Akiyama makes a comment regarding Silvia's skill at stealing, which causes Sally to feel suffocated. Amidst the social tension, Colin McNaughton announces his intention to marry Silvia after his studies, sparking a debate among the students regarding marriage and age. Celia expresses her belief that the recent incidents involving ink and a backpack will be resolved by the following day.

The next morning, Miss Lemon arrives late to meet Hercule Poirot, breathless and flustered. She informs him that Celia has committed suicide, likely via a morphine overdose, leaving a note behind. Poirot instructs Miss Lemon to file his letters while he travels to Chestnut Street. Upon his arrival, the servant Jerome recognizes Poirot from the previous evening and conspiratorially informs him that Celia died in her bed. Jerome adds that she had been engaged to Mr. Collins, a tall man who smokes a pipe, just the night before. Jerome offers to notify the mistress once the police have finished their investigation.
